1.name: Rage
1.description: In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.description: While you are not wearing any armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.

2.name: Reckless Attack
2.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with fierce desperation.

2.name: Danger Sense
2.description: You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see.

3.name: Primal Path
3.description: You choose a path that shapes the nature of your rage. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

5.name: Fast Movement
5.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

6.name: Path Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Primal Path.

7.name: Feral Instinct
7.description: You have advantage on initiative rolls. If surprised at the beginning of combat and not incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn if you enter your rage first.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Brutal Critical
9.description: You can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.

10.name: Path Feature
10.description: You gain a feature from your Primal Path.

11.name: Relentless Rage
11.description: Your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw to drop to 1 hit point instead.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Brutal Critical (2 dice)
13.description: You can roll two additional weapon damage dice when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.

14.name: Path Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Primal Path.

15.name: Persistent Rage
15.description: Your rage ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Brutal Critical (3 dice)
17.description: You can roll three additional weapon damage dice when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.

18.name: Indomitable Might
18.description: If your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Primal Champion
20.description: Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music.

1.name: Bardic Inspiration
1.description: You can inspire others through stirring words or music. A creature gains a Bardic Inspiration die (d6) to add to an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

2.name: Jack of All Trades
2.description: You can add half your proficiency bonus to any ability check that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

2.name: Song of Rest (d6)
2.description: You can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest, granting extra 1d6 hit points.

3.name: Bard College
3.description: You delve into the advanced techniques of a bard college of your choice. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th and 14th level.

3.name: Expertise
3.description: Choose two skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for ability checks using those proficiencies.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
5.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d8. You also regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

5.name: Font of Inspiration
5.description: You regain all expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: Countercharm
6.description: As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn to counter mind-influencing effects on friendly creatures within 30 feet.

6.name: Bard College Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Bard College.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Song of Rest (d8)
9.description: The extra hit points from Song of Rest increases to 1d8.

10.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
10.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d10.

10.name: Expertise
10.description: Choose two more skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for ability checks using those proficiencies.

10.name: Magical Secrets
10.description: Choose two spells from any class, including this one. These count as bard spells for you.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Song of Rest (d10)
13.description: The extra hit points from Song of Rest increases to 1d10.

14.name: Magical Secrets
14.description: Choose two additional spells from any class.

14.name: Bard College Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Bard College.

15.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
15.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d12.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Song of Rest (d12)
17.description: The extra hit points from Song of Rest increases to 1d12.

18.name: Magical Secrets
18.description: Choose two additional spells from any class.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Superior Inspiration
20.description: When you roll initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: As a conduit for divine power, you can cast cleric spells.

1.name: Divine Domain
1.description: Choose one domain related to your deity. Your choice grants domain spells and other features.

2.name: Channel Divinity (1/rest)
2.description: You gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from your deity, starting with Turn Undead and an effect from your domain.

2.name: Divine Domain Feature
2.description: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Destroy Undead (CR 1/2)
5.description: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, the creature is instantly destroyed if its CR is at or below 1/2.

6.name: Channel Divinity (2/rest)
6.description: You can now use Channel Divinity twice between rests.

6.name: Divine Domain Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Destroy Undead (CR 1)
8.description: You can now destroy undead of CR 1 or lower with Turn Undead.

8.name: Divine Domain Feature
8.description: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

10.name: Divine Intervention
10.description: You can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf. Roll percentile dice; if you roll equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes.

11.name: Destroy Undead (CR 2)
11.description: You can now destroy undead of CR 2 or lower with Turn Undead.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Destroy Undead (CR 3)
14.description: You can now destroy undead of CR 3 or lower with Turn Undead.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Destroy Undead (CR 4)
17.description: You can now destroy undead of CR 4 or lower with Turn Undead.

17.name: Divine Domain Feature
17.description: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

18.name: Channel Divinity (3/rest)
18.description: You can now use Channel Divinity three times between rests.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Divine Intervention Improvement
20.description: Your Divine Intervention automatically succeeds, no roll required.

1.name: Druidic
1.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak it and use it to leave hidden messages.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will.

2.name: Wild Shape
2.description: You can use your action to magically assume the shape of a beast that you have seen before.

2.name: Druid Circle
2.description: You choose to identify with a circle of druids. Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

4.name: Wild Shape Improvement
4.description: You can now use Wild Shape to transform into beasts with a swim speed.

6.name: Druid Circle Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Wild Shape Improvement
8.description: You can now use Wild Shape to transform into beasts with a flying speed.

10.name: Druid Circle Feature
10.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Druid Circle Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Timeless Body
18.description: The primal magic you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year.

18.name: Beast Spells
18.description: You can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Archdruid
20.description: You can use Wild Shape an unlimited number of times. You can also ignore verbal and somatic spell components, as well as material components without a cost.

1.name: Fighting Style
1.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose from Archery, Defense, Dueling, Great Weapon Fighting, Protection, or Two-Weapon Fighting.

1.name: Second Wind
1.description: You can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once per short or long rest.

2.name: Action Surge
2.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action. Once per short or long rest.

3.name: Martial Archetype
3.description: You choose an archetype that you strive to emulate in your combat styles and techniques. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th levels.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Ability Score Improvement
6.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

7.name: Martial Archetype Feature
7.description: You gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Indomitable (1/rest)
9.description: You can reroll a saving throw that you fail. You must use the new roll. Once per long rest.

10.name: Martial Archetype Feature
10.description: You gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

11.name: Extra Attack (2)
11.description: You can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Indomitable (2/rest)
13.description: You can now use Indomitable twice between long rests.

14.name: Ability Score Improvement
14.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

15.name: Martial Archetype Feature
15.description: You gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Action Surge (2/rest)
17.description: You can now use Action Surge twice before a rest, but still only once per turn.

17.name: Indomitable (3/rest)
17.description: You can now use Indomitable three times between long rests.

18.name: Martial Archetype Feature
18.description: You gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Extra Attack (3)
20.description: You can attack four times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.description: While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

1.name: Martial Arts
1.description: Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use unarmed strikes and monk weapons.

2.name: Ki
2.description: Your training allows you to harness the mystic energy of ki, represented by ki points equal to your monk level.

2.name: Unarmored Movement
2.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield.

3.name: Monastic Tradition
3.description: You commit yourself to a monastic tradition. Your tradition grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 11th, and 17th level.

3.name: Deflect Missiles
3.description: You can use your reaction to deflect or catch the missile when you are hit by a ranged weapon attack.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

4.name: Slow Fall
4.description: You can use your reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage by an amount equal to five times your monk level.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

5.name: Stunning Strike
5.description: When you hit another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a stunning strike.

6.name: Ki-Empowered Strikes
6.description: Your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

6.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

7.name: Evasion
7.description: Your instinctive agility lets you dodge out of the way of certain area effects, like a dragon's breath or a fireball.

7.name: Stillness of Mind
7.description: You can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Unarmored Movement Improvement
9.description: You gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.

10.name: Purity of Body
10.description: Your mastery of the ki flowing through you makes you immune to disease and poison.

11.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
11.description: You gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Tongue of the Sun and Moon
13.description: You learn to touch the ki of other minds so that you understand all spoken languages. Any creature that can understand a language can understand what you say.

14.name: Diamond Soul
14.description: Your mastery of ki grants you proficiency in all saving throws. If you fail, you can spend 1 ki point to reroll and take the second result.

15.name: Timeless Body
15.description: Your ki sustains you so that you suffer none of the frailty of old age, and you can't be aged magically.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
17.description: You gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

18.name: Empty Body
18.description: You can use your action to spend 4 ki points to become invisible for 1 minute and gain resistance to all damage except force.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Perfect Self
20.description: When you roll for initiative and have no ki points remaining, you regain 4 ki points.

1.name: Divine Sense
1.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect the presence of strong evil or good forces within 60 feet.

1.name: Lay on Hands
1.description: You have a pool of healing power equal to 5 times your paladin level. You can restore hit points from this pool as an action.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.description: You adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose from Defense, Dueling, Great Weapon Fighting, or Protection.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.description: You have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and prayer to cast spells.

2.name: Divine Smite
2.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal extra radiant damage.

3.name: Divine Health
3.description: The divine magic flowing through you makes you immune to disease.

3.name: Sacred Oath
3.description: You swear the oath that binds you as a paladin forever. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th and 20th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Aura of Protection
6.description: Whenever you or a friendly creature within 10 feet must make a saving throw, the creature gains a bonus equal to your Charisma modifier.

7.name: Sacred Oath Feature
7.description: You gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Aura of Courage
10.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet can't be frightened while you are conscious.

11.name: Improved Divine Smite
11.description: Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Cleansing Touch
14.description: You can use your action to end one spell on yourself or on one willing creature that you touch.

15.name: Sacred Oath Feature
15.description: You gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Aura Improvements
18.description: The range of your Aura of Protection and Aura of Courage increases to 30 feet.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Sacred Oath Feature
20.description: You gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

1.name: Favored Enemy
1.description: You have significant experience studying, tracking, hunting, and even talking to a certain type of enemy. Choose a type of favored enemy.

1.name: Natural Explorer
1.description: You are particularly familiar with one type of natural environment and adept at traveling and surviving in such regions.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose from Archery, Defense, Dueling, or Two-Weapon Fighting.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.description: You have learned to use the magical essence of nature to cast spells.

3.name: Ranger Archetype
3.description: You choose an archetype that you strive to emulate. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th levels.

3.name: Primeval Awareness
3.description: You can expend one spell slot to sense certain creature types within 1 mile for 1 minute per spell level expended.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Favored Enemy Improvement
6.description: You choose one additional favored enemy, as well as an associated language.

6.name: Natural Explorer Improvement
6.description: You choose one additional favored terrain.

7.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
7.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Land's Stride
8.description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed.

10.name: Natural Explorer Improvement
10.description: You choose one additional favored terrain.

10.name: Hide in Plain Sight
10.description: You can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself using naturally occurring materials, gaining +10 to Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

11.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
11.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Vanish
14.description: You can use the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn. You also can't be tracked by nonmagical means unless you choose to leave a trail.

14.name: Favored Enemy Improvement
14.description: You choose one additional favored enemy, as well as an associated language.

15.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
15.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Feral Senses
18.description: You gain preternatural senses. When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Foe Slayer
20.description: Once on each of your turns, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the attack roll or the damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies.

1.name: Expertise
1.description: Choose two skill proficiencies or one skill proficiency and thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for those ability checks.

1.name: Sneak Attack
1.description: Once per turn, you can deal extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit if you have advantage on the attack roll or if another enemy of the target is adjacent.

1.name: Thieves' Cant
1.description: You learned thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code allowing you to hide messages in conversation.

2.name: Cunning Action
2.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to take a bonus action to Dash, Disengage, or Hide on each of your turns in combat.

3.name: Roguish Archetype
3.description: You choose an archetype that you emulate. Your archetype choice grants you features at 3rd level and then again at 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

3.name: Sneak Attack (2d6)
3.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 2d6.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Uncanny Dodge
5.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

5.name: Sneak Attack (3d6)
5.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 3d6.

6.name: Expertise
6.description: Choose two more skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for those ability checks.

6.name: Sneak Attack (4d6)
6.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 4d6.

7.name: Evasion
7.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success, and only half damage on a failure.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Sneak Attack (4d6)
8.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 4d6.

9.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
9.description: You gain a feature from your Roguish Archetype.

9.name: Sneak Attack (5d6)
9.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 5d6.

10.name: Ability Score Improvement
10.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Sneak Attack (5d6)
10.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 5d6.

11.name: Reliable Talent
11.description: Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

11.name: Sneak Attack (6d6)
11.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 6d6.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

12.name: Sneak Attack (6d6)
12.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 6d6.

13.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
13.description: You gain a feature from your Roguish Archetype.

13.name: Sneak Attack (7d6)
13.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 7d6.

14.name: Blindsense
14.description: If you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet of you.

14.name: Sneak Attack (7d6)
14.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 7d6.

15.name: Slippery Mind
15.description: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

15.name: Sneak Attack (8d6)
15.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 8d6.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

16.name: Sneak Attack (8d6)
16.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 8d6.

17.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
17.description: You gain a feature from your Roguish Archetype.

17.name: Sneak Attack (9d6)
17.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 9d6.

18.name: Elusive
18.description: No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

18.name: Sneak Attack (9d6)
18.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 9d6.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

19.name: Sneak Attack (10d6)
19.description: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 10d6.

20.name: Stroke of Luck
20.description: If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Or if you fail an ability check, treat the d20 roll as a 20. Use once per short or long rest.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with arcane magic that fuels your spells.

1.name: Sorcerous Origin
1.description: Choose a sorcerous origin describing the source of your innate magical power. Your choice grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

2.name: Font of Magic
2.description: You tap into a deep wellspring of magic within yourself, represented by sorcery points that allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

3.name: Metamagic
3.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Metamagic
10.description: You learn one additional Metamagic option.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Metamagic
17.description: You learn one additional Metamagic option.

18.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
18.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Sorcerous Restoration
20.description: You regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.

1.name: Otherworldly Patron
1.description: You have struck a bargain with an otherworldly being of your choice. Your choice grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

1.name: Pact Magic
1.description: Your arcane research and the magic bestowed on you by your patron have given you facility with spells.

2.name: Eldritch Invocations
2.description: You have unearthed eldritch invocations, fragments of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability. You gain two eldritch invocations of your choice.

3.name: Pact Boon
3.description: Your otherworldly patron bestows a gift upon you for your loyal service. Choose from Pact of the Chain, Pact of the Blade, or Pact of the Tome.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Otherworldly Patron.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
10.description: You gain a feature from your Otherworldly Patron.

11.name: Mystic Arcanum (6th level)
11.description: Your patron bestows upon you a magical secret. Choose one 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Mystic Arcanum (7th level)
13.description: Your patron gives you a magical secret. Choose one 7th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.

14.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Otherworldly Patron.

15.name: Mystic Arcanum (8th level)
15.description: Your patron gives you a magical secret. Choose one 8th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Mystic Arcanum (9th level)
17.description: Your patron gives you a magical secret. Choose one 9th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Eldritch Master
20.description: You can spend 1 minute entreating your patron to regain all expended spell slots from your Pact Magic feature. Once per long rest.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have a spellbook containing spells that show the first glimmerings of your true power.

1.name: Arcane Recovery
1.description: Once per day when you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level no greater than half your wizard level.

2.name: Arcane Tradition
2.description: You choose an arcane tradition, shaping your practice of magic through one of eight schools. Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
6.description: You gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
10.description: You gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
14.description: You gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Spell Mastery
18.description: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose one 1st-level and one 2nd-level wizard spell in your spellbook; you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Signature Spells
20.description: Choose two 3rd-level wizard spells as your signature spells. You always have them prepared and can cast each once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot.
